tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README introduces vikunja-mcp as a Model Context Protocol server that lets AI assistants interact with Vikunja task management instances. It documents all 11 tool categories (tasks, projects, labels, teams, users, filters, templates, webhooks, batch import, export, buckets), two authentication methods (API token and JWT), and the project's enterprise-grade security and reliability features. This is the roadmap for everything the tour will explore in code.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: src/index.ts is the main entry point that bootstraps the entire MCP server. It creates an McpServer with stdio transport, initializes the AuthManager and VikunjaClientFactory, registers all tools via the central registry, and auto-authenticates from environment variables if available. This single file orchestrates the startup sequence that connects every subsystem examined in later steps.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript async IIFE pattern: the main() function is defined as async and immediately invoked to allow top-level await for server initialization, while also catching and logging any fatal errors.

tour.title: Authentication System
tour.description: The AuthManager is the most-imported module in the codebase (50 fan-in edges), because every tool must verify authentication before operating. It handles session lifecycle (connect, disconnect, status), auto-detects whether a token is an API token (tk_*) or JWT (eyJ*) by format, and provides test-only methods guarded by environment checks. The permissions module maps each tool to required auth types, gating JWT-only tools like users and export.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated auth types: the AuthManager uses token format pattern matching to determine auth capabilities at runtime, then the PermissionManager uses a static map (TOOL_PERMISSIONS) to enforce which tools are available for each auth type.

tour.title: Client Management
tour.description: Building on the AuthManager from Step 3, the client layer manages connections to the Vikunja API. src/client.ts provides a thread-safe singleton ClientContext with mutex-protected factory injection, while VikunjaClientFactory creates and caches API client instances, automatically recreating them when credentials change. With 64 fan-in edges, client.ts is the second most depended-upon module -- nearly every tool needs it to make API calls.

tour.title: Tool Registration Hub
tour.description: src/tools/index.ts is the central registry with the highest fan-out in the codebase (23 outgoing edges), importing every tool module and conditionally registering them on the MCP server. Tools requiring JWT auth (users, export) are only registered when the authentication type permits it. This file is the architectural pivot point: the entry point (Step 2) calls it, and it calls out to all 17+ tool modules explored in subsequent steps.
tour.languageLesson: Conditional registration pattern: tools are registered based on runtime auth state rather than compile-time configuration, allowing the same server binary to expose different tool sets depending on how the user authenticates.

tour.title: Type System Foundation
tour.description: src/types/index.ts has 88 fan-in edges, making it the most depended-upon module in the entire project. It is a barrel file re-exporting all domain model types: Vikunja entities (tasks, projects, labels, teams, users), error types (MCPError, ErrorCode), filter types, response structures, and MCP tool context interfaces. The vikunja.ts module defines the complete Vikunja domain model covering every API entity, while errors.ts provides the structured MCPError class used for error handling throughout the codebase.

tour.title: Task CRUD Operations
tour.description: Task management is the core purpose of this server. The task-crud.ts tool registers the vikunja_task_crud MCP tool, routing create/get/update/delete/list subcommands to dedicated service modules. TaskCreationService handles task creation with label and assignee management plus automatic rollback on failure. TaskUpdateService performs diff-based updates with state analysis and label/assignee synchronization. These services use retry logic with circuit breakers for resilience.

tour.title: Filtering and Storage
tour.description: The filtering subsystem is one of the most architecturally significant parts of the project, spanning multiple strategy implementations. SimpleFilterStorage provides session-isolated, mutex-protected in-memory filter storage in just 393 lines -- replacing a previous 33-file, 9800-line system. The filter utilities in src/utils/filters.ts use Zod-based validation and a recursive descent parser to convert filter strings into structured FilterExpression objects, with security validation against injection attacks.
tour.languageLesson: Strategy pattern in TypeScript: the filtering system defines a TaskFilteringStrategy interface and provides three implementations (ServerSide, ClientSide, Hybrid). The FilteringContext selects the appropriate strategy at runtime, enabling transparent fallback from server-side to client-side filtering when the Vikunja API cannot handle a particular filter expression.

tour.title: Security and Validation
tour.description: Security is layered throughout the codebase rather than isolated in a single module. src/utils/validation.ts provides comprehensive input sanitization against XSS, SQL injection, command injection, path traversal, and prototype pollution using Zod schemas. src/utils/security.ts handles credential masking and URL sanitization to prevent exposure in logs and error reports. Together with the Zod-based filter validation from Step 8, these utilities form a defense-in-depth architecture.

tour.title: Middleware Layer
tour.description: The middleware layer enforces rate limiting and permission checking before tool handlers execute. The rate limiter uses express-rate-limit with mutex-protected concurrent access and opossum circuit breakers for fail-safe operation. It categorizes tools into four tiers (default, expensive, bulk, export) with separate limits for each. The direct-middleware module composes permission checking and rate limiting in the correct order -- permissions first, then rate limiting -- wrapping every tool handler.

tour.title: Resilience Infrastructure
tour.description: The retry system in src/utils/retry.ts replaces 580+ lines of custom logic with the battle-tested opossum circuit breaker library. It provides configurable retry policies with exponential backoff, a shared circuit breaker registry (so related operations share failure state), transient error detection, and pre-configured profiles for task and bulk operations. Memory protection in src/utils/memory.ts uses V8-specific estimation algorithms to prevent unbounded resource consumption during bulk operations.
tour.languageLesson: Circuit breaker pattern: when a Vikunja API endpoint fails repeatedly, the circuit 'opens' and immediately rejects requests for a cooldown period, preventing cascading failures. After the reset timeout, it enters 'half-open' state and allows a test request through to determine if the service has recovered.

tour.title: Entity Management Tools
tour.description: Beyond tasks, the server provides tools for managing all Vikunja entities. Projects follow a modular architecture with separate CRUD, hierarchy, sharing, and validation modules. Labels, teams, users, and webhooks each follow the same subcommand-based pattern established by the task tools: a Zod validation schema defines the input, subcommands route to handlers, and responses use the standardized formatting. These tools collectively implement the full breadth of the Vikunja API surface.

tour.title: Configuration Management
tour.description: The ConfigurationManager is a singleton that detects the runtime environment (dev/test/prod), merges profile defaults with environment variables, and validates everything through Zod schemas. It provides typed accessors for auth configuration, logging levels, and rate limit settings per tool category. The .env.example file documents all 27 configuration variables, serving as both reference and template.

tour.title: CI/CD and Quality Gates
tour.description: Three GitHub Actions pipelines enforce quality at every stage. The CI pipeline runs lint, typecheck, and test coverage across a Node.js 20.x/22.x matrix on every push and pull request. The release pipeline builds, tests, and publishes to npm when a GitHub release is created. The security pipeline runs weekly npm audit and CodeQL static analysis. Together with the 90%+ branch coverage requirement enforced by Jest, these pipelines ensure the enterprise-grade reliability promised in the README.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions matrix strategy: the CI workflow uses a version matrix to test against multiple Node.js versions in parallel, catching compatibility issues early. The release workflow gates npm publishing behind successful test execution using job dependencies.
